summary.intent: Implement the Timer specialized component (task #294) in the ars-ui Rust codebase, including the state machine, effects, tests, spec conformance, and property-based tests.
summary.outcome: The Timer component was fully implemented using Duration types throughout, all 46 unit/snapshot tests pass, proptest invariants pass, spec was synchronized to match the codebase's Effect enum pattern, doc warnings were resolved, coverage hit 99.10% region, and the work was committed and a PR pushed.

repo: The Effect pattern uses a typed Effect enum with marker effects (e.g., DurationTimer, TimerInterval) owned by the adapter—not string names or set_interval/cancel_interval APIs. Adapters implement the recurring timer themselves.
initial_effects (overriding take_initial_effects) is the canonical way to emit mount-time effects like starting a timer interval; cancel_effect is used to cancel them.
CLAUDE.md mandates resolving spec/code drift in the same PR—spec sections must be updated to match the implemented pattern.
Spec snippet compilation uses a pure syn syntax check (no type or lint checking), so API example blocks are valid as long as they parse.
Unreferenced insta snapshot warnings from bare `cargo insta test` without --features are a pre-existing artifact of i18n-feature-gated snapshots, not a real issue.
Module-level //! doc comments cannot link to enum variants (e.g., Effect::TimerInterval)—only the enum type itself resolves. Variant references must be demoted to plain code spans.

The convention for time-related fields is core::time::Duration, not u32/u64 milliseconds. search_input::Props.debounce and scroll_area::Props.hide_delay already use Duration; clipboard/toast/navigation_menu are older and out of scope.
Clippy lints enabled: cast_lossless, uninlined_format_args, missing_const_for_fn. cast_precision_loss is NOT enabled, so u64 as f64 is allowed.

code.finding: SetTime is an unconditional context-sync event (mirrors clipboard/toast pattern), so it can set a nonzero current while the machine is in the Completed state. Proptest per-step invariants must allow for this.
Canonical reference for the typed Effect enum + marker effect + initial_effects + cancel_effect pattern that Timer was required to follow.
SetTime invariant had to be relaxed after proptest found that Completed state can have nonzero current due to unconditional context sync.
The spec snippet compilation gate is a pure syn syntax check with no type or lint checking, so spec code blocks only need to parse, not compile cleanly.

workflow: Running clippy with --all-targets catches test-code lints (unused imports, let _ drops) that per-crate clippy misses.
xci-fast is the pre-push gate (~10-15 min); it runs 10 gates including coverage checks and must pass before committing/pushing.
Parallel agent exploration of component structure, core APIs, and test patterns simultaneously is effective for large codebase onboarding before writing code.
Proptest is a useful complement to snapshot tests—it surfaced a real semantic edge case (unconditional SetTime in Completed state) that handwritten unit tests missed.
Coverage reports can pinpoint uncovered production lines; the only acceptable uncovered region is no-op test callback code per CLAUDE.md.
Saving durable conventions (e.g., Duration over _ms integers) to memory files ensures future components default to the correct pattern without repeating the discovery.

friction: Spec/code drift: the spec used nonexistent set_interval/cancel_interval APIs and string effect names instead of the typed Effect enum pattern—required significant investigation before writing any code.
Module-level //! doc comments cannot link to enum variants, requiring two rounds of doc warning fixes to demote variant links to plain code spans.
Clippy flagged Idle→Start and Paused→Resume/Start match arms as identical duplicate arms, requiring a match restructure mid-implementation.
Proptest surfaced a real semantic issue with the SetTime invariant that required understanding the unconditional context-sync pattern before the fix was obvious.
i18n-feature-gated snapshot warnings from bare `cargo insta test` are a pre-existing noise source in test output that can be mistaken for real failures.
open_items: Older components (clipboard, toast, navigation_menu) still use _ms integer fields for time—migration to Duration is out of scope for this PR but noted as technical debt.
Timer adapter implementations (the code that actually owns the recurring interval in each platform adapter) were not part of this PR scope.
PlatformEffects::set_timeout still takes delay_ms: u32—a candidate for Duration migration in a future sweep.
